name: Valle del Cuco – circular desde Encinas de Esgueva
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Encinas De Esgueva traverse the Esgueva Valley, a region characterized by its river, varied terrain, and historical elements. The area sits at the confluence of the provinces of Valladolid, Burgos, and Palencia, offering a mix of landscapes from flat valley paths to more undulating routes. Key features include the Esgueva River, which provides accessible "Green Paths," and the Encinas Reservoir, surrounded by loop trails and Mediterranean scrubland. The elevation changes within the Páramos de Esgueva comarca contribute to diverse cycling experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Encinas De Esgueva The most popular touring cycling route is Valle del Cuco – circular desde Curiel de Duero, a 27.3 miles (43.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 39 minutes to complete, offering views of the Esgueva Valley. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Valle del Cuco – circular desde Encinas de Esgueva, a moderate 36.5 miles (58.8 km) path. This route explores the valley's diverse terrain, including areas with holm oaks and Mediterranean scrubland. Local touring cyclists also love the Iglesia de Torresandino – Villafruela loop from La Calzada, a 19.8 miles (31.9 km) trail leading through agricultural landscapes and small villages, often completed in about 2 hours 49 minutes. Touring cycling around Encinas De Esgueva is defined by the Esgueva River valley, the Encinas Reservoir, and undulating terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from gentle valley paths to routes with more significant elevation gain. The routes in Encinas De Esgueva are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 14 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Encinas De Esgueva's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Encinas De Esgueva?
A: There are 5 touring cycling routes available around Encinas De Esgueva, offering a range of experiences for cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in the Encinas De Esgueva region?
A: The region offers diverse terrain, from flat "Green Paths" along the Esgueva River to more undulating routes as you explore the wider Páramos de Esgueva comarca. You'll encounter scenic river valleys, areas with holm oaks, and Mediterranean scrubland.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Encinas De Esgueva?
A: While all listed routes are rated moderate or difficult, the Esgueva Valley features accessible "Green Paths" that are often flat and suitable for touring cyclists of varying skill levels. These paths follow the Esgueva River, providing a gentle cycling experience.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, for experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40379765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iglesia de Torresandino – Villafruela loop from La Calzada&lt;/a&gt; is rated as difficult. Other moderate routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1175303676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle del Cuco – circular desde Encinas de Esgueva&lt;/a&gt; also feature significant elevation gain, providing a good workout.

Q: What natural features can I see while cycling around Encinas De Esgueva?
A: You'll encounter the scenic Esgueva River valley, the Embalse de Encinas (Encinas Reservoir) which is a popular spot for recreation, and diverse ecosystems featuring riparian vegetation, Mediterranean scrubland with holm oaks, thyme, rosemary, and lavender. The reservoir area is also rich in birdlife.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or cultural sites along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You can explore the majestic 14th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4384200" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Zúñiga Family (Encinas de Esgueva)&lt;/a&gt;, the 15th-century Gothic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5576204" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción&lt;/a&gt;, and the charming village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5506336" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tórtoles de Esgueva&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5507727" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Curiel de Duero Castle&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable nearby attraction.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1175293169" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle del Cuco – circular desde Curiel de Duero&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1175303676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle del Cuco – circular desde Encinas de Esgueva&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40379768" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Encinas Reservoir – Encinas Reservoir loop from Villovela de Esgueva&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Encinas De Esgueva?
A: The Esgueva Valley is generally pleasant for cycling. Spring and autumn offer milder temperatures and beautiful scenery, making them ideal. Summers can be warm, but early mornings or late afternoons are still suitable. The varied terrain means you can find comfortable routes in most seasons.

Q: What do other cyclists say about the touring routes in Encinas De Esgueva?
A: The touring cycling routes around Encinas De Esgueva are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 6 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from river valleys to areas with holm oaks, and the opportunity to explore historical villages.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the Encinas Reservoir?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40379768" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Encinas Reservoir – Encinas Reservoir loop from Villovela de Esgueva&lt;/a&gt; is a dedicated route that explores the surroundings of the Embalse de Encinas, offering scenic views and opportunities for other activities like swimming or fishing.

Q: Can I find routes that connect different villages in the Esgueva Valley?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for connecting various villages. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31483511" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guzmán – Tórtoles de Esgueva loop from Encinas de Esgueva&lt;/a&gt; allow you to cycle through agricultural landscapes and charming small towns, experiencing the local culture and beekeeping tradition.

Q: Where can I find more information about the Esgueva Valley for cycling?
A: For additional information on cycling in the Esgueva Valley, you can consult resources like &lt;a href="https://www.visitarvalladolid.com/ruta-btt-valle-esgueva/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitarvalladolid.com&lt;/a&gt;, which provides details on routes like the Ruta BTT Valle Esgueva.
tips: Built on the Esgueva River, this reservoir is used mainly for irrigation and supplying the towns of the region. The reservoir offers a beautiful panoramic view and is a popular place for outdoor activities.

It is a charming church, cared for with care, and which includes curious architecture with two types of Gothic, two very simple Gothic portals, a Plateresque altarpiece, a curious Holy Thursday monument... We were lucky to find it open, but there will be to ask in town who can teach it

Built in the 14th century, the fortification was part of the defensive line of the Esgueva and had the objective of protecting the entrance to the valley. It has gone through different owners throughout its history, including the Marqués de Lorca. In the 1970s, it was used as a grain store, but it is currently restored and empty inside, under the ownership of the Encinas Town Hall.
